description_long: This object is a vintage folding bellows camera, likely from the early to mid-20th century, featuring a rectangular box-shaped body with a collapsible, accordion-style bellows extending from the front. The camera's exterior is covered in a textured, dark material, possibly leather or leatherette, with visible metal hardware and fasteners. The front standard holds a circular lens assembly, surrounded by a metal frame, and includes a viewfinder and adjustment knobs. The base plate is flat and metallic, with mechanical components for supporting and adjusting the camera's position. The top of the camera has a carrying handle and a latch mechanism for securing the camera when folded. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, with signs of wear and patina consistent with age and use.
description: A vintage folding bellows camera with a textured dark exterior, metal hardware, and an accordion-style lens assembly. The camera features a boxy body, a circular lens, and a flat metal base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the extended bellows, textured body, and metal base plate with visible fasteners.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular lens, viewfinder, and the accordion bellows extending from the camera body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the bellows extended and the camera's mechanical supports visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the open back of the camera and the full length of the bellows, with the interior visible.
